title: Mac & Dennis Move to the Suburbs

jokes.text: DEE: It's not your bed, okay? It's my goddamn floor!
jokes.setup: Mac complains about Dee's feet on his bed
jokes.punchline: Dee reveals they're sleeping on her floor, not their bed
jokes.explanation: Sharp reversal that establishes their pathetic living situation. Good economy and misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: It's not your bed, okay? It's my goddamn floor!

jokes.text: You're turning this place into the Superdome.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about Mac and Dennis trashing her place
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the disaster relief conditions at the Superdome
jokes.explanation: Decent reference but Dennis immediately calls it out as dated and lame, which is the actual joke.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: You're turning this place into the Superdome.

jokes.text: Superdome? That's, like, six years too late. That's a super lame reference. And it's kind of offensive.
jokes.setup: Dee makes the Superdome reference
jokes.punchline: Dennis critiques her reference as outdated and offensive
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about comedy writing itself. Very Dennis to be pretentiously critical.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Superdome? That's, like, six years too late. That's a super lame reference. And it's kind of offensive.

jokes.text: Oh, I take that as a compliment. It's not a compliment. Well, it's biblical, so it's a compliment.
jokes.setup: Dee calls them locusts as an insult
jokes.punchline: Mac takes it as a compliment because it's 'biblical'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - anything biblical must be good. Shows his character's warped religious thinking.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Oh, I take that as a compliment.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: It's not a compliment.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Well, it's biblical, so it's a compliment.

jokes.text: I wish we hadn't burned down our rent-controlled apartment. (both laugh)
jokes.setup: Complaining about expensive rent
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions they burned down their apartment and laugh about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sunny dark humor - casually mentioning arson like it's a minor mistake they laugh about.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I wish we hadn't burned down our rent-controlled apartment.
(both laugh)

jokes.text: The one where the family was murdered? Brutally murdered. Pass.
jokes.setup: Charlie suggests moving to empty apartment
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of brutal murder, then Mac's understated 'Pass'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation followed by Mac's hilariously understated response. Good structure.

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: The one where the family was murdered? Brutally murdered.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Pass.

jokes.text: And we don't have to worry about getting ringworm. You brought the ringworm.
jokes.setup: Dennis says they won't get ringworm at new place
jokes.punchline: Dee reveals Dennis brought the ringworm originally
jokes.explanation: Simple reversal joke. Dennis trying to blame others for problems he caused.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And we don't have to worry about getting ringworm.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You brought the ringworm.

jokes.text: These are city scum. They can't hack it out here.
jokes.setup: Frank observing Mac and Dennis
jokes.punchline: Frank calling them 'city scum' when he's equally terrible
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Frank is equally awful. Sets up the bet premise.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: These are city scum. They can't hack it out here.

jokes.text: And if you don't, you have to sleep in bed with an old man for a year. (laughing): Gross! What?
jokes.setup: Frank proposing bet terms
jokes.punchline: Absurd punishment of sleeping with old man, Dennis's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot point with appropriately absurd stakes for this show.

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: And if you don't, you have to sleep in bed with an old man for a year.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: (laughing): Gross! What?

jokes.text: You already sleep with an old man, and for no reason other than you seem to enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Charlie laughing at the bet
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out Charlie already sleeps with Frank
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their weird relationship dynamic. 'Seem to enjoy it' is perfectly acidic Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You already sleep with an old man, and for no reason other than you seem to enjoy it.

jokes.text: I call it Mac's famous mac and cheese. (both laughing)
jokes.setup: Mac serves dinner he worked hard on
jokes.punchline: It's just mac and cheese but he calls it 'famous'
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag. Mac's desperate need for validation through food.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: I call it Mac's famous mac and cheese.

jokes.text: I don't really know how famous it is. I mean, I'm your roommate, and I've never heard of it.
jokes.setup: Mac calling his mac and cheese 'famous'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's logical deconstruction of the claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - pedantic and deflating Mac's enthusiasm with cold logic.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: I don't really know how famous it is. I mean, I'm your roommate, and I've never heard of it.

jokes.text: (crickets trilling)
jokes.setup: They toast to a good night's sleep in peaceful suburbs
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to loud cricket sounds that will torment them
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - cuts directly from their toast to the noise that will destroy their peace.

jokes.text: (smoke detector chirping)
jokes.setup: Crickets were already annoying
jokes.punchline: Now there's also a smoke detector chirping
jokes.explanation: Escalates the suburban noise torture. Sets up Mac's denial about hearing it.

jokes.text: Pool filter? Oh, my God. It was like a jet engine.
jokes.setup: Mac asks about pool filter noise
jokes.punchline: Dennis's dramatic description of how loud it was
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly suburban paradise is falling apart. Dennis's typical dramatic overstatement.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Pool filter?

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. It was like a jet engine.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a long commute, so... Yeah, I was thinking more, like, Bryan Adams.
jokes.setup: Mac made a Creed mix for commute
jokes.punchline: Dennis wants Bryan Adams instead
jokes.explanation: Shows their different terrible music tastes and suburban stress already causing friction.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's a long commute, so...

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was thinking more, like, Bryan Adams.

jokes.text: Name's Wally Schmidt. No relation to Mike. But don't tell my wife. (chuckles)
jokes.setup: Neighbor introduces himself
jokes.punchline: Weird joke about not being related to Mike Schmidt that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect suburban dad humor - confusing reference that makes him chuckle at his own joke.

dialogue.speaker: Wally
dialogue.line: Name's Wally Schmidt. No relation to Mike. But don't tell my wife. (chuckles)

jokes.text: Boy, it sure is a hot one today, huh?
jokes.setup: Wally meeting the new neighbors
jokes.punchline: Classic meaningless weather small talk
jokes.explanation: Sets up Wally's running gag of stating obvious weather facts. Will pay off later.

dialogue.speaker: WALLY
dialogue.line: Boy, it sure is a hot one today, huh?

jokes.text: Just comes up and starts talking to us? Right, some guy comes up to me, I don't know what he's... I don't know what he's up to.
jokes.setup: Normal neighbor interaction
jokes.punchline: Dennis is paranoid about basic friendliness
jokes.explanation: Shows how city paranoia doesn't translate to suburbs. Pure Dennis sociopathy.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Just comes up and starts talking to us? Right, some guy comes up to me, I don't know what he's... I don't know what he's up to.

jokes.text: I'm staring at him, I'm thinking, does he have a knife? Are we gonna have to take this bitch out right now?
jokes.setup: Dennis being suspicious of neighbor
jokes.punchline: Escalates to considering murdering friendly neighbor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis psychopath humor. Escalates from suspicion to murder over friendly small talk.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: I'm staring at him, I'm thinking, does he have a knife? Are we gonna have to take this bitch out right now?

jokes.text: Come on, you got, you have to seize the goddamn gap! People are so goddamn inefficient!
jokes.setup: Dennis in traffic during commute
jokes.punchline: Road rage over normal traffic behavior
jokes.explanation: 'Seize the gap' becomes quotable Dennis road rage. Shows suburban commute stress.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Come on, you got, you have to seize the goddamn gap! People are so goddamn inefficient!

jokes.text: You old fat bitch! You fat bitch!
jokes.setup: Dennis already angry at traffic
jokes.punchline: Escalates to screaming insults at elderly driver
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's rage escalation but feels a bit lazy compared to 'seize the gap.'

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You old fat bitch! You fat bitch!

jokes.text: But it turns out, it's actually really complicated, and I didn't go to school for that, you know?
jokes.setup: Dennis asks if Mac fixed the pool filter
jokes.punchline: Mac's excuse that he didn't go to school for it
jokes.explanation: Mac's logic for avoiding responsibility. 'Didn't go to school for that' becomes a running excuse.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: But it turns out, it's actually really complicated, and I didn't go to school for that, you know?

jokes.text: It took a little while to find a stud.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his day setting up living room
jokes.punchline: Picture shows TV awkwardly mounted way too high
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of incompetent home improvement. The 'stud' double meaning is unintentional by Mac.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: It took a little while to find a stud.

jokes.text: (low hissing) Shut up, shut up, shut up, shut up! (chirping) Aw, damn it! What is that?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to sleep peacefully
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown over suburban night sounds
jokes.explanation: Shows escalating psychological breakdown from what city dwellers think will be 'peaceful'

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Shut up, shut up, shut up, shut up!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Aw, damn it! What is that?

jokes.text: Use your signal, you cow! (tires screech) Goddamn it.
jokes.setup: Dennis commuting home in traffic
jokes.punchline: More road rage with animal insults
jokes.explanation: Continues road rage theme. 'Cow' fits with other animal insults but not as memorable.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Use your signal, you cow! (tires screech) Goddamn it.

jokes.text: Slow down! Children play here, you fat cow!
jokes.setup: Dennis has been road raging and speeding
jokes.punchline: Suddenly concerned about children's safety while calling someone a 'fat cow'
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy - Dennis pretending to care about safety while being abusive. Good ironic structure.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Slow down! Children play here, you fat cow!

jokes.text: Hot one today, huh? Yeah?!
jokes.setup: Wally gives his standard weather greeting
jokes.punchline: Dennis's aggressive response to innocent small talk
jokes.explanation: Callback to Wally's weather obsession meets Dennis's deteriorating mental state. Good escalation.

dialogue.speaker: WALLY
dialogue.line: Hot one today, huh?

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Yeah?!

jokes.text: Oh, that Wally loves to say 'It's a hot one' when I already know it's a hot one. 'Cause I'm standing outside in the hot one.
jokes.setup: Dennis comes inside after seeing Wally
jokes.punchline: Obsessive analysis of Wally's weather small talk
jokes.explanation: Dennis's obsessive breakdown of normal social interaction. 'In the hot one' is perfectly awkward phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Oh, that Wally loves to say 'It's a hot one' when I already know it's a hot one. 'Cause I'm standing outside in the hot one.

jokes.text: Tell me about future weather. Don't tell me about present weather...
jokes.setup: Dennis ranting about Wally's weather comments
jokes.punchline: Demanding Wally predict future weather instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. Dennis's mental breakdown creates genuinely funny surreal demand.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Tell me about future weather. Don't tell me about present weather...

jokes.text: This is Jimmy-- he's the pool guy. I invited him over for dinner.
jokes.setup: Dennis comes home stressed from commute
jokes.punchline: Mac casually mentions he invited a stranger for dinner
jokes.explanation: Sets up Dennis's explosive reaction. Mac's obliviousness to social norms.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: This is Jimmy-- he's the pool guy. I invited him over for dinner.

jokes.text: You gonna invite some guy over to my house that I don't even know.
jokes.setup: Mac invited pool guy for dinner
jokes.punchline: Dennis's escalating anger at basic social situation
jokes.explanation: Dennis's control issues and antisocial tendencies emerging under suburban stress.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You gonna invite some guy over to my house that I don't even know.

jokes.text: If he knew shit that I was interested in, he wouldn't be a (bleep) pool guy.
jokes.setup: Mac suggests Dennis could talk to pool guy
jokes.punchline: Dennis's classist dismissal of pool guy's worth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis elitism and condescension. Reveals his true character through class prejudice.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: If he knew shit that I was interested in, he wouldn't be a (bleep) pool guy.

jokes.text: Here I am thinking that we got a pool that's outside. I didn't realize that we had an indoor pool.
jokes.setup: Dennis is still angry about dinner situation
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about Mac 'flooding' the house
jokes.explanation: Dennis's passive-aggressive sarcasm about Mac's domestic incompetence.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Here I am thinking that we got a pool that's outside. I didn't realize that we had an indoor pool.

jokes.text: And yet we don't have enough money for a recipe book, 'cause here we are eating mac and cheese again.
jokes.setup: Dennis sarcastically talking about having two pools
jokes.punchline: Bitter complaint about eating mac and cheese again
jokes.explanation: Callback to the mac and cheese. Shows Dennis's growing resentment of their domestic situation.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: And yet we don't have enough money for a recipe book, 'cause here we are eating mac and cheese again.

jokes.text: You don't know how hard it is to be cooped up in here all by myself, okay?
jokes.setup: Dennis complaining about having to work
jokes.punchline: Mac complaining about being 'cooped up' in large suburban house
jokes.explanation: Ironic complaint about being stuck in their suburban paradise. Sets up role reversal.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: You don't know how hard it is to be cooped up in here all by myself, okay?

jokes.text: I get to go to work? Oh, everybody, hey, I 'get' to go to work. I 'get' to have a job.
jokes.setup: Mac says Dennis gets to go to work
jokes.punchline: Dennis's sarcastic breakdown about having to work
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic meltdown. Good use of air quotes sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: I get to go to work? Oh, everybody, hey, I 'get' to go to work. I 'get' to have a job.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure it's a cricket.
jokes.setup: Dennis has been going crazy over mysterious chirping
jokes.punchline: Mac reveals he knew what it was all along
jokes.explanation: Payoff to running gag. Mac's casual revelation after Dennis's suffering.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: I'm pretty sure it's a cricket.

jokes.text: How are they gonna find an old man that's gonna go along with it? Right. Exactly.
jokes.setup: Dennis doubting Frank can find old man for bet
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by doorbell and reveal
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing setup for the reveal. Classic 'speak of the devil' structure.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: How are they gonna find an old man that's gonna go along with it?

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Right. Exactly.

jokes.text: Mac and Dennis, old man. Old man, Mac and Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dennis just said it would be impossible to find an old man
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact introduction of old man
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and Frank's casual presentation of absurd situation. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Mac and Dennis, old man. Old man, Mac and Dennis.

jokes.text: And it is famous. In my stomach.
jokes.setup: Frank asks about Mac's famous mac and cheese
jokes.punchline: Charlie's literal interpretation of 'famous'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's weird logic about fame. Good callback with Charlie-specific absurdist twist.

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: And it is famous. In my stomach.

jokes.text: I mean, we're your friends, I've never heard of it.
jokes.setup: Charlie saying mac and cheese is famous
jokes.punchline: Dee repeating Dennis's earlier logic
jokes.explanation: Straight callback to earlier joke. Less effective second time but maintains running gag.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: I mean, we're your friends, I've never heard of it.

jokes.text: I'll take that California king bed for myself.
jokes.setup: Side bet about whether Mac and Dennis will succeed
jokes.punchline: Dee's greedy grab for the bed
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's opportunistic nature. Sets up her involvement in the bet.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'll take that California king bed for myself.

jokes.text: Fine, and if they lose, the three of you got to sleep with the old man.
jokes.setup: Dee wants side action on the bet
jokes.punchline: Frank expands punishment to include all three of them
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurd stakes. Frank's casual cruelty expanding the punishment.

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: Fine, and if they lose, the three of you got to sleep with the old man.

jokes.text: I have to piss. Out of my penis.
jokes.setup: Dennis excusing himself from awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily anatomical clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis awkwardness. Unnecessarily specific biological detail shows his mental breakdown.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: I have to piss. Out of my penis.

jokes.text: Look, dude, I don't want to sound racist, but can we just discuss the fact that it's an old black man?
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis discussing the bet situation
jokes.punchline: Mac's 'not to sound racist' followed by immediately sounding racist
jokes.explanation: Classic 'I'm not racist but...' structure. Shows Mac's oblivious prejudice.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Look, dude, I don't want to sound racist, but can we just discuss the fact that it's an old black man?

jokes.text: That's two things, it's two things.
jokes.setup: Mac realizing old man is also black
jokes.punchline: Mac's panicked counting of 'problems'
jokes.explanation: Mac's panic at multiple 'complications.' Shows his mental math about awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: That's two things, it's two things.

jokes.text: Why am I in the middle? Black man should be in the middle.
jokes.setup: Discussing sleeping arrangement logistics
jokes.punchline: Mac's racist bed arrangement suggestions
jokes.explanation: Continues the uncomfortable racial discussion. Mac's casual racism emerging under stress.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Why am I in the middle? Black man should be in the middle.

jokes.text: Old man is down at the bottom, sideways, like a dog.
jokes.setup: Still discussing bed arrangements
jokes.punchline: Mac suggesting old man sleep 'like a dog'
jokes.explanation: Mac's dehumanizing suggestion escalates the uncomfortable racism.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Old man is down at the bottom, sideways, like a dog.

jokes.text: You guys are cracking, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis having breakdown about sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Dee realizes they're already defeated
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment. Dee's opportunistic recognition of their weakness.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: You guys are cracking, aren't you?

jokes.text: It's soul-crushing, Dee! Oh, my God, I can't make this house into a home. I didn't go to school for that.
jokes.setup: Dee asking if they're cracking
jokes.punchline: Mac's dramatic confession about domestic failure
jokes.explanation: Callback to Mac's earlier excuse. 'Soul-crushing' is perfectly dramatic for Mac.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: It's soul-crushing, Dee! Oh, my God, I can't make this house into a home. I didn't go to school for that.

jokes.text: You got me a dog? Yeah. Oh, my God, he's so cute!
jokes.setup: Dennis reveals he got Mac a dog
jokes.punchline: Mac's childlike excitement over the gift
jokes.explanation: Mac's genuine childlike joy contrasts with their usual cynicism. Sets up dark turn.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: You got me a dog?

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Oh, my God, he's so cute!

jokes.text: I'm gonna name him Dennis. Huh? You're Dennis. He's Dennis Jr.
jokes.setup: Mac excited about naming the dog
jokes.punchline: Names dog after Dennis, creating awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Awkward intimacy of naming dog after your friend. Dennis's discomfort is palpable.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: I'm gonna name him Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Huh?

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: You're Dennis. He's Dennis Jr.

jokes.text: I'm gonna raise this guy like he was our own son.
jokes.setup: Mac bonding with the dog
jokes.punchline: Mac treating dog ownership like parenthood with Dennis
jokes.explanation: Mac's fantasy of domestic partnership with Dennis. Makes Dennis uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: I'm gonna raise this guy like he was our own son.

jokes.text: But you don't really know what hot is, do you? Hot's a storm. You ever been in a storm, Wally?
jokes.setup: Wally's innocent weather comment
jokes.punchline: Dennis's unhinged metaphorical threat
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis psychological breakdown - menacing monologue to innocent neighbor

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: But you don't really know what hot is, do you? Hot's a storm. You ever been in a storm, Wally?

jokes.text: You ever been in a storm, Wally? I mean, a real storm? Not a thunderstorm, but a storm of fists
jokes.setup: Wally making innocent weather small talk
jokes.punchline: Dennis's violent metaphorical 'storm' threatening Wally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from weather talk to violence threat. Perfectly crafted menacing monologue that's become iconic.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You ever been in a storm, Wally? I mean, a real storm? Not a thunderstorm, but a storm of fists

jokes.text: raining down on your head. Blasting you in the face. Pummeling you in the stomach. Hitting you in the chest so hard you think your heart's gonna stop.
jokes.setup: Dennis started storm metaphor
jokes.punchline: Increasingly violent and specific threats
jokes.explanation: Continuation of storm speech. Escalating violence with poetic language. Peak Dennis psychopath moment.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: raining down on your head. Blasting you in the face. Pummeling you in the stomach. Hitting you in the chest so hard you think your heart's gonna stop.

jokes.text: (screaming)
jokes.setup: Dennis's threatening storm monologue
jokes.punchline: Wally's terrified scream reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat. Wally's terror is the punchline to Dennis's monologue. Great economy.

jokes.text: No one. What's that? Dog grave.
jokes.setup: Mac asks who Dennis was talking to
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that the dog is dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy economy. Casual revelation of dog's death in two words. Sets up cannibal twist.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: No one.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: What's that?

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Dog grave.

jokes.text: everything I do, I do for you!
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis fighting about their domestic situation
jokes.punchline: Mac's dramatic declaration of devotion
jokes.explanation: Mac's codependent dramatic outburst. Shows his emotional desperation and need for Dennis's approval.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: everything I do, I do for you!

jokes.text: Sleeping in your car. Eating at Applebee's.
jokes.setup: Mac accusing Dennis of being selfish
jokes.punchline: Reveals Dennis has been avoiding home by sleeping in car and eating at Applebee's
jokes.explanation: Great revelation of Dennis's pathetic avoidance. Applebee's is perfectly sad choice for Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Sleeping in your car. Eating at Applebee's.

jokes.text: You put meat hunks in it! Wow! Hunks of meat! Mmm!
jokes.setup: Mac says Dennis didn't notice he changed the mac and cheese
jokes.punchline: Dennis's sarcastic excitement about 'meat hunks'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's sarcastic appreciation. 'Hunks of meat' becomes darkly ironic given what follows.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You put meat hunks in it! Wow! Hunks of meat! Mmm!

jokes.text: Well, of course Dennis would like Dennis. What? It's Dennis! What are you talking about? You're eating the dog!
jokes.setup: Dennis enjoying the meat in mac and cheese
jokes.punchline: Mac reveals Dennis is eating the dog named after him
jokes.explanation: Peak Sunny darkness. Cannibalism reveal with the twist that he's eating his namesake. Brilliant structure and maximum shock value.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Well, of course Dennis would like Dennis. What? It's Dennis! What are you talking about? You're eating the dog!

jokes.text: (laughing wildly)
jokes.setup: Mac reveals Dennis ate the dog
jokes.punchline: Mac's unhinged laughter at his own cruelty
jokes.explanation: Mac's psychotic break. The wild laughter shows how suburban stress has destroyed his sanity.

jokes.text: I don't know! I guess it was just a cry for attention!
jokes.setup: Dennis asking what's wrong with Mac
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual explanation for feeding Dennis dog meat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement. Treating dog cannibalism as just seeking attention is perfectly Mac.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: I don't know! I guess it was just a cry for attention!

jokes.text: You didn't even blink twice when I told you that the dog was dead!
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his motivation
jokes.punchline: Expecting sympathy for dead dog announcement
jokes.explanation: Mac's twisted logic about Dennis's reaction to pet death

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You didn't even blink twice when I told you that the dog was dead!

jokes.text: I called it my famous mac and cheese as-as a joke. But then it seemed like you like it. You like that it was Mac's famous mac and cheese.
jokes.setup: Dennis demanding explanation about mac and cheese
jokes.punchline: Mac's pathetic confession about the 'famous' lie
jokes.explanation: Payoff to running gag. Shows Mac's desperate need for validation and how far he'll go for approval.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: I called it my famous mac and cheese as-as a joke. But then it seemed like you like it. You like that it was Mac's famous mac and cheese.

jokes.text: You're the one that's sitting at Applebee's eating pot stickers all by yourself. You're the one that's pathetic!
jokes.setup: Dennis calling Mac pathetic
jokes.punchline: Mac's comeback about Dennis's sad Applebee's habit
jokes.explanation: Good escalation. Pot stickers at Applebee's is perfectly sad detail for Dennis's avoidance.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: You're the one that's sitting at Applebee's eating pot stickers all by yourself. You're the one that's pathetic!

jokes.text: News flash, asshole! I've been hearing it the entire goddamn time!
jokes.setup: Dennis asking about chirping sound
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals he's been hearing it all along
jokes.explanation: Great payoff. Dennis's explosive confession that he's been suffering in silence. 'News flash, asshole!' is pure Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: News flash, asshole! I've been hearing it the entire goddamn time!

jokes.text: We're gonna win the bet! Free rent for a year! We did it! Free rent for... Wait. Wait, wait, wait, wait. Free rent for where?
jokes.setup: Celebrating winning the bet
jokes.punchline: Realizing they won rent for a place they hate
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic realization. They 'won' something they don't want. Great structure with excitement turning to horror.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: We're gonna win the bet! Free rent for a year! We did it!

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Free rent for... Wait. Wait, wait, wait, wait. Free rent for where?

jokes.text: Sorry, black man. Old man. Old man. Sorry, old black man. Goddamn it.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to be polite to the old man
jokes.punchline: Charlie's inability to not mention race despite trying
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier racial awkwardness. Charlie's attempt at politeness makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: Sorry, black man. Old man. Old man. Sorry, old black man. Goddamn it.
